rulings.Case Title: TERRENCE BARNUM VS. PAUL RYAN ASSOCIATES INC., et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Furthere Response To Form Interrogatories-Employment Law (Set One), And Req. For Production Of Documents (Set One)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, December 17, 2014, Line 2: PLAINTIFF TERRENCE BARNUM'S Motion To Compel Further Response To Form Interrogatories-Employment Law (Set One), And Request For Production Of Documents (Set One). Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear, but are ordered to meet and confer in advance of the hearing in an effort to resolve the two remaining issues and to agree on narrowed language for Special Interrogatory No. 1. If counsel are unable to agree on narrowed language, each party is directed to present at the hearing its proposed modification to the Special Interrogatory No. 1, in writing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to katherinehuibonhoa@paulhastings.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CARMEN DIAZ ECHAURI VS. EKO VENTURE, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Deft'S Further Response To Req.S For Production, Set Two And Req. For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, December 17, 2014, Line 10, PLAINTIFF CARMEN ECHAURI'S Amended Motion To Compel Defendant's Further Response To Requests For Production, Set Two And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Hearings will begin at 10:00 a.m. on December 17, 2014. Motion denied. The timeline in question is classic attorney work product. It was created by the defense attorney based on her review of the file. She avers that the timeline also contains her written impressions and notes. Where there is no question as to whether the subject is attorney work product the Court is statutorily denied the power to order its production for in camera review. (See Costco Wholesale Corp. v. Sup. Ct. (2009) 47 Cal.4th 725, 739.) Further, the deposition testimony reflects an immediate objection to any testimony regarding the document, which precludes a finding of waiver. Plaintiff shall pay sanctions in the amount of $2,940 to Defendant within 30 days from entry of this Order. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: D CAMERON BAKER VS. PEOPLE WITH DISABILITIES FOUNDATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, December 17, 2014, Line 12, CROSS-DEFENDANT D BAKER'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT. Hearings will begin at 10:00 a.m. on December 17, 2014. Sustained without leave to amend. As to the second cause of action for fraud, the First Amended Cross-Complaint (FACC) fails to sufficiently allege the elements of justifiable reliance and damages. The FACC also lacks the requisite specificity. (See Lazar v. Sup. Ct. (1996) 12 Cal.4th 631, holding that the particularity requirement for fraud "necessitates pleading facts which show how, when, where, to whom, and by what means the representations were tendered.") As to the third and fourth causes of action, the FACC fails to allege facts establishing that Cross-Complainant acted to its detriment because of the alleged duress and undue influence, in a way that can be reconciled with the statement in the Cross-Complaint that Cross-Complainant "refused" to give in to Cross-Defendant's alleged demands. (Cross-Complaint, par. 17.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: BENJAMIN RICHEY ET AL VS. JUSTIN D GIARLA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction - Opposition Due On: 12/03/14; Reply Due On: 12/10/14
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, December 17, 2014, Line 14, Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction. Hearings will begin at 10:00 a.m. on December 17, 2014. The OSC re preliminary injunction is granted. The court enjoins defendant and his agents and others acting in concert with him from selling or offering to sell plaintiff's artwork or representing plaintiff in any capacity. Defendant does not oppose this relief. The court further enjoins defendant from transferring, encumbering, selling or hypothecating any part or the whole of real property located at 866 Geary Street. Plaintiff demonstrates a likelihood of prevailing on his breach of trust and breach of fiduciary duty causes of action. The evidence demonstrates that defendant failed to turn over all funds that were owed to plaintiff and used those funds to purchase the building. Plaintiff shall post a $20,000 undertaking per CCP 529. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number.
